md:
**Recruiting Is Being Run Backwards**
-------------------------------------

For decades, recruiting has followed the same basic playbook.

A job opens.

A description is written.

The job is posted to a board.

Recruiters wait for applicants.

This model treats hiring like a classified ad.

It assumes the right candidates will appear at the exact moment a role becomes available.

But the world no longer works that way.

Today, candidates engage with companies long before they apply for a job. They follow brands, read content, attend events, and build familiarity with organizations over time.

Yet most recruiting systems ignore those relationships entirely.

Instead of building pipelines of interested candidates, companies repeatedly start from zero every time a job opens.

The result is slow hiring, overwhelmed HR teams, and an endless cycle of sourcing and screening.

Recruiting has become reactive.

**Hiring Should Start With Relationships**
------------------------------------------

Modern marketing doesn’t start with a transaction.

It starts with a relationship.

Companies build audiences, nurture interest, and guide people through thoughtful journeys before asking for a decision.

Recruiting should work the same way.

Candidates are not just applicants. They are people who develop interest in a company over time.

Some follow a brand on social media.

Some apply but are not selected.

Some engage with content or events.

These signals represent real candidate interest.

But without a system to organize and nurture those relationships, that interest is lost.

Every hiring cycle begins from scratch.

**The Real Problem Is Fragmentation**
-------------------------------------

Most mid-market HR teams are forced to assemble recruiting workflows from a patchwork of tools.

*   An ATS to track applicants.
    
*   A sourcing tool to find candidates.
    
*   A scheduling tool to coordinate interviews.
    
*   An engagement platform to send messages.
    

Each tool solves a small problem, but none organize the entire system.

The result is fragmented workflows, scattered candidate data, and HR leaders constantly switching between systems just to keep hiring moving.

This fragmentation creates operational drag and prevents teams from building lasting relationships with candidates.

**CandidateHub Organizes Candidate Relationships**
--------------------------------------------------

CandidateHub exists to solve this problem.

Instead of treating candidates as one-time applicants, CandidateHub helps organizations manage ongoing candidate relationships.

The platform centralizes candidate engagement, communication, and hiring workflows so teams can see and manage their entire talent pipeline in one place.

CandidateHub organizes candidate relationships and journeys, allowing HR teams to move from reactive recruiting to structured talent pipelines.

Recruiters gain visibility into who is engaged, where candidates are in the journey, and when to reconnect.

Hiring becomes organized, visible, and repeatable.

**Intelligence for Teams That Can’t Hire a Recruitment Marketing Department**
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------

Large enterprises solve recruiting complexity by hiring entire recruitment marketing teams.

Most mid-market organizations cannot.

That is why CandidateHub includes Markis.

Markis acts as an embedded recruitment marketing intelligence system, helping companies design candidate journeys, automate engagement, and build pipelines without requiring a dedicated marketing team.

Together, CandidateHub and Markis allow organizations to operate modern recruiting systems without the complexity or cost typically required.

**The Future of Recruiting**
----------------------------

Recruiting should not start with a job posting.

It should start with a relationship.

Organizations that build and nurture candidate relationships over time will hire faster, attract better candidates, and reduce the operational chaos that overwhelms HR teams today.

CandidateHub exists to make that possible.

For years, recruiting has been measured by volume.

*   More applicants.
    
*   More data.
    
*   More signals.
    
*   More automation.
    
      
    

On paper, that should have made hiring easier.

In reality, many teams are quietly feeling the opposite:

Less confidence.

Less control.

More legal questions they’re not equipped to answer.

We’re entering what I’d call a **recruitment data reckoning** — and it’s forcing teams to rethink a belief that’s gone largely unquestioned:

**That more candidates automatically means better hiring.**

  

**When “Helpful Data” Becomes a Risk**
--------------------------------------

Recent events have pulled the curtain back on something many teams assumed was safe.

The lawsuit involving [Eightfold AI](chatgpt://generic-entity?number=0) has put a spotlight on how candidate data is assembled, enriched, and ultimately used to evaluate people.

This isn’t about one vendor.

It’s about a broader industry pattern.

Many recruiting tools now:

*   Assemble data from multiple sources
    
*   Enrich candidate profiles beyond what the candidate explicitly provided
    
*   Use that data to score, rank, or evaluate people
    
      
    

The uncomfortable question is no longer _“Is this effective?”_

It’s _“Are we actually allowed to do this?”_

In most organizations, recruiters didn’t consent candidates for these downstream uses.

Legal teams didn’t sign off on the nuance.

And yet — the liability doesn’t sit with “the platform.”

It sits with the employer.

  

**Platform Data vs. First-Party Trust**
---------------------------------------

At the same time, job platforms are asking for more operational data back from employers.

[Indeed](chatgpt://generic-entity?number=1)’s push to collect deeper disposition data is a good example.

On the surface, it sounds harmless. Helpful, even.

But it represents a subtle shift:

Control over candidate data is moving _away_ from the hiring team and _toward_ the platform.

Once that happens:

*   You don’t fully own the relationship
    
*   You don’t fully control how signals are interpreted
    
*   You’re exposed to policy changes you didn’t agree to
    
      
    

That’s not a technical problem.

That’s a governance problem.

  

**The Fake Candidate Problem Nobody Wants to Talk About**
---------------------------------------------------------

Then there’s the issue everyone _feels_ but struggles to quantify.

Fraudulent candidates.

Bots.

Identity masking.

Entire pipelines polluted with people who were never viable to begin with.

The cost isn’t just recruiter time.

Bad data trains bad systems.

Bad systems reinforce bad decisions.

And suddenly:

*   Your funnel metrics look “healthy”
    
*   Your hiring outcomes feel worse
    
*   And no one trusts the data anymore
    
      
    

Volume didn’t help.

It hid the problem.

  

**The Shift That’s Starting to Happen**
---------------------------------------

Quietly, a different mindset is emerging.

Recruiting leaders are asking:

*   “Do we actually need _more_ candidates?”
    
*   “Or do we need _better signal_?”
    
*   “If Legal asked us to explain our candidate data tomorrow, could we?”
    
      
    

This is where the conversation changes.

The safest hiring systems moving forward won’t be the ones with the most data.

They’ll be the ones with the **clearest permission, cleanest signal, and strongest audit trail**.

Consent-based engagement isn’t a limitation.

It’s becoming a form of risk management.

  

**Calm Is the New Competitive Advantage**
-----------------------------------------

This moment doesn’t require panic.

It requires restraint.

Recruiting teams don’t need:

*   Louder tools
    
*   Bigger funnels
    
*   More enrichment
    
      
    

They need systems that assume scrutiny — from candidates, from regulators, from leadership.

Systems built on:

*   Candidate-initiated engagement
    
*   Explicit consent
    
*   First-party data
    
*   Fewer candidates, higher confidence
    
*     
    

That’s not slower hiring.

That’s calmer hiring.

And in a market flooded with noise, calm is starting to look like a competitive advantage.

  

If this topic is already coming up in your organization — even quietly — you’re not alone.

Happy to compare notes with anyone navigating these questions right now.

_No pitch. Just an important conversation._

This post was inspired by a thoughtful LinkedIn post from Jen Warrington on the difference between job descriptions and job postings. If you haven’t read it, it’s worth your time. ([click here](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/jenwarrington_ahhhh-%F0%9D%97%B7%F0%9D%97%BC%F0%9D%97%AF-%F0%9D%97%B1%F0%9D%97%B2%F0%9D%98%80%F0%9D%97%B0%F0%9D%97%BF%F0%9D%97%B6%F0%9D%97%BD%F0%9D%98%81%F0%9D%97%B6%F0%9D%97%BC%F0%9D%97%BB%F0%9D%98%80-%F0%9D%98%83%F0%9D%98%80-activity-7422635036132425729-2N5k?utm_source=share&utm_medium=member_desktop&rcm=ACoAAALRoHoBKHq27ka2KcKEalkhsdC5CWv3upI))

Jen makes a clear and important distinction:

*   **Job descriptions** are internal HR documents designed for structure, compensation, and clarity.
    
*   **Job postings** are external-facing tools meant to attract talent and tell a story.
    

She’s absolutely right. And yet, I think there’s an even deeper issue hiding in plain sight — one that quietly impacts how recruiters write, publish, and think about job postings altogether.

### The Subtle Mindset Shift That Changes Everything

Many recruiters still view a job posting as a _document_.

Not intentionally. Not incorrectly. But unconsciously.

When something is framed as a document, we optimize for:

*   Completeness
    
*   Accuracy
    
*   Risk reduction
    
*   Internal alignment
    

All important things. But candidates don’t experience job postings as documents.

They experience them as **decision pages**.

And that single mindset shift changes how job postings should be written, structured, and evaluated.

Candidates Don’t Read Job Postings. They Scan for Signals.
----------------------------------------------------------

A candidate landing on a job posting is not settling in with a coffee to read every word.

They’re skimming. Fast.

They’re asking themselves, often subconsciously:

*   Is this role for someone like me?
    
*   Do I understand what success looks like here?
    
*   Can I see myself working with this team?
    
*   Is this worth my time to apply?
    

If the posting leads with a long list of requirements, internal language, or compliance-heavy bullets, most candidates never make it far enough to answer those questions.

This is where many job postings quietly fail — not because the role isn’t good, but because the _experience_ isn’t compelling.

A Job Posting Should Behave Like a Landing Page
-----------------------------------------------

Think about how modern landing pages work.

They don’t start with disclaimers.  
They don’t lead with fine print.  
They don’t immediately ask for commitment.

They guide a decision.

A strong job posting should do the same.

### 1\. Lead With the Outcome, Not the Title

Instead of opening with:

> “We are hiring a Senior Operations Manager…”

Try starting with:

> “Help scale a growing team without sacrificing how work gets done.”

The job title still matters. It just doesn’t need to be the hook.

### 2\. Describe the Reality of the Role

Candidates want honesty, not perfection.

Instead of dumping responsibilities, paint a picture:

*   What does a typical day look like?
    
*   Who do they work with?
    
*   What decisions do they own?
    
*   What problems will they be trusted to solve?
    

Clarity builds confidence. Confidence drives applications.

### 3\. Translate Requirements Into Signals

You still need must-haves. They matter.

But how they’re framed matters more.

Instead of:

*   5+ years experience
    
*   Strong communication skills
    
*   Ability to work in a fast-paced environment
    

Try:  
**This role is likely a great fit if you’ve…**

*   Led projects end-to-end and owned outcomes
    
*   Communicated complex ideas to different stakeholders
    
*   Thrived in environments where things are evolving, not rigid
    

Same filter. Far better experience.

### 4\. Sell the Environment, Not Just the Role

People don’t leave jobs because of tasks.  
They leave because of environments.

Strong job postings answer questions like:

*   How are people supported?
    
*   How is success measured?
    
*   How do managers show up?
    
*   What happens when things go wrong?
    

This is where trust is built.

### 5\. Put the “Must-Haves” at the Bottom

Always.

By the time a candidate reaches the requirements section, they should already be thinking:

> “I hope I qualify.”

That’s the moment where intent converts into action.

Where CandidateHub Fits Into This Philosophy
--------------------------------------------

At CandidateHub, we believe job postings shouldn’t exist in isolation.

They’re not static documents.  
They’re not one-and-done listings.  
They’re the _front door_ to a longer relationship with talent.

That’s why we focus on:

*   Turning job interest into ongoing engagement
    
*   Capturing intent, not just applications
    
*   Nurturing candidates who aren’t ready _yet_, but will be
    

When job postings are treated like landing pages, they naturally fit into a broader candidate journey — one where attraction, engagement, and conversion are connected, not siloed.

Final Thought
-------------

Jen is absolutely right: job descriptions and job postings are not the same thing.

But the real opportunity for recruiters lies in going one step further.

Stop thinking of job postings as documents.  
Start thinking of them as experiences.

Because candidates already are.

In today’s competitive market, titles and résumés don’t tell the full story. Two candidates may share the same job title, yet bring completely different abilities to the table. That’s why forward-thinking organizations are moving away from traditional hiring and embracing a skills-based approach.

At the heart of this approach are four essential questions every leader must answer:

1.  **What skills do we need?**  
    Business needs evolve quickly. The skills that mattered yesterday may not be the ones you’ll rely on tomorrow. Understanding market trends and future demand is critical.
    
2.  **What skills do we have?**  
    Many organizations don’t have a clear view of their internal talent. Without visibility, strengths go underutilized, and employees feel overlooked.
    
3.  **What skills can we develop?**  
    Your next leaders may already be on your team — but only if you’re developing them. Succession planning is no longer optional; it’s essential to retaining talent and preparing for growth.
    
4.  **What skills do we need to acquire?**  
    Some gaps can only be closed by recruiting externally. The key is spotting those needs early so you can hire proactively, not reactively.
    

Why Ignoring Skill Gaps is Costly
---------------------------------

Without a skills-based strategy, hiring becomes guesswork. That leads to:

*   Roles filled by the wrong people.
    
*   Missed opportunities to promote from within.
    
*   Turnover as employees leave for growth opportunities elsewhere.
    
*   Higher recruitment costs and longer time-to-fill.
    

It’s like trying to fill a leaking bucket — no matter how much effort you put in, talent continues to slip away.

From Gaps to Growth
-------------------

The companies that succeed aren’t always the ones with the deepest pockets. They’re the ones that understand their skill gaps — and act quickly to close them.

By mapping the skills you already have, identifying those you can grow, and planning for the ones you’ll need to bring in, you build a foundation for stronger, more resilient teams. This approach doesn’t just improve hiring — it strengthens retention, accelerates development, and positions your business for long-term success.

How CandidateHub Helps
----------------------

CandidateHub was built to give leaders the clarity they need to make skills-based hiring a reality. Our platform provides a **real-time snapshot of your workforce** and uses AI-driven engagement to help you:

*   See exactly where your gaps are.
    
*   Develop your internal talent with clear succession paths.
    
*   Recruit with precision when external skills are needed.
    

And it all starts with a **free Skills Gap Analysis**.

Take the First Step
-------------------

You can’t build a championship team on guesswork. You need to know what skills matter, where you stand today, and how to close the gaps that hold you back.

That’s the power of a Skills Gap Analysis — and we’re offering it at no cost.

You’ll walk away with actionable insights you can use immediately, whether you choose to work with us or not.

👉 Book your **free Skills Gap Analysis** today and take the first step toward building a team that wins.

Hiring has never been harder. Markets are shifting, technology is evolving faster than most organizations can keep up with, and traditional recruitment methods simply don’t deliver the results they once did.

The companies that thrive in this environment are the ones that move away from hiring based on titles or résumés — and instead focus on **skills**.

The Four Questions Every Leader Must Ask
----------------------------------------

A truly skills-based hiring strategy starts by answering four essential questions:

1.  **What skills do we need?**  
    Business needs evolve. The skills that made you successful last year may not be the ones you’ll need 18 months from now. A market view helps you stay ahead.
    
2.  **What skills do we have?**  
    Most organizations don’t have a clear picture of their internal talent. Identifying strengths (and gaps) is the foundation of smarter workforce planning.
    
3.  **What skills can we develop?**  
    Your future leaders may already be on your team. Succession planning and targeted development ensure you don’t lose great talent or get caught unprepared when key people move on.
    
4.  **What skills do we need to acquire?**  
    Some gaps can only be closed with external recruitment. Knowing what to hire for — before the need becomes urgent — saves time, money, and frustration.
    

The Cost of Ignoring Skill Gaps
-------------------------------

Companies that don’t measure skills often rely on guesswork. That leads to:

*   Roles filled by the wrong candidates.
    
*   Missed opportunities to promote from within.
    
*   Rising turnover when employees don’t see a growth path.
    
*   Slower hiring cycles and higher recruitment costs.
    

It’s the equivalent of trying to fill a leaking bucket — no matter how much you pour in, talent keeps slipping through the cracks.

How CandidateHub Helps Close the Gaps
-------------------------------------

This is where CandidateHub comes in.

Our platform gives you a **real-time snapshot** of your workforce: what you have, what you’re missing, and where you need to grow. With AI-powered engagement, personalized campaigns, and candidate journeys, CandidateHub helps you build a clear, skills-driven hiring and development strategy.

And the best part? You can start with a **free Skills Gap Analysis**.

You’ll walk away with a detailed view of:

*   The strengths your team already has.
    
*   The gaps that could hold you back.
    
*   Clear next steps to develop, promote, or recruit with purpose.
    

Build Teams That Win
--------------------

Championship teams aren’t built on luck — they’re built on knowing exactly what skills are needed, where they exist, and how to close the gaps.

If you’re ready to move from guesswork to growth, now is the time.

👉 Book your **free Skills Gap Analysis** today and take the first step toward building the kind of team that wins.

Imagine making efficient hiring decisions with confidence, securing the best candidates quickly and effectively.

This guide will walk you through how CandidateHub can transform your recruitment process and position your organization for success in this dynamic environment.

### Unlocking Intelligent Candidate Matching:

  
Unlocking intelligent candidate matching is a cornerstone of CandidateHub's capabilities. Transforms how your recruiters connect with potential hires by analyzing vast datasets to identify candidates who best meet job requirements. Through sophisticated campaigns, CandidateHub analyzes skills, experience, and even engagement, ensuring you spend time only on the most promising candidates.

Imagine sifting through thousands of candidates effortlessly, with the system highlighting those who not only qualify but are also highly interested in your company. This process reduces time-to-hire significantly and enhances decision-making accuracy.

By leveraging our platform, recruiters can confidently make informed hiring decisions, securing the best candidates with precision and speed. This is how CandidateHub redefines recruitment efficiency and effectiveness.

### Streamlining the Screening Process

Streamlining the screening process is crucial for efficient hiring, and CandidateHub's AI capabilities excel in this area. By automating initial candidate screening, the platform saves recruiters significant time and effort. CandidateHub evaluates applications against predefined criteria, quickly filtering out unsuitable candidates and highlighting those who meet the essential qualifications.

This automated screening ensures that only the most relevant candidates advance to further rounds, allowing recruiters to focus on personalized interactions and in-depth evaluations. The system's ability to learn and adapt means it continually improves its screening accuracy, resulting in better candidate pools over time.

By optimizing the screening process, CandidateHub allows recruiters to handle more applications without compromising quality. This efficiency leads to a faster hiring cycle and ensures that your organization secures top talent promptly.

###   
Boost Efficiency with Automation Tools

Boosting efficiency with automation tools is at the heart of CandidateHub's approach to recruitment. By automating routine tasks, recruiters can redirect their focus toward strategic activities like candidate engagement and relationship building. CandidateHub's automation tools handle everything from responding to candidates engaging with your brand to following up with candidates already in the pipeline, ensuring a seamless recruitment process.

These tools not only save time but also minimize human error, providing a consistent and personalized experience for candidates. Automated workflows can be tailored to specific roles or departments, allowing for a customized approach that meets your organization's unique needs.

By leveraging these automation capabilities, recruiters can significantly increase their productivity and ensure that no candidate falls through the cracks. This efficiency in managing recruitment tasks results in quicker placements and a more dynamic hiring process.

### Simplify Communication and Scheduling

Simplifying communication and scheduling is essential for a smooth recruitment process, and CandidateHub's automation tools excel in these areas.

The platform automates candidate communications, ensuring timely updates and feedback. This keeps candidates engaged and informed throughout the recruitment journey, enhancing their experience.

Automated scheduling further eases the process by coordinating interviews without the usual back-and-forth emails. CandidateHub integrates with calendar systems to provide candidates with available slots, allowing them to select times that suit their schedules. This reduces administrative overhead and accelerates interview timelines.

By streamlining communication and scheduling, recruiters can focus on building relationships and assessing candidate potential rather than managing logistics. This not only saves time but also ensures a professional and organized recruitment process, ultimately leading to a more effective hiring strategy.

### Gain Insights for Better Decisions

Gaining insights for better decisions is a pivotal advantage of using CandidateHub's data analytics features. The platform provides comprehensive analytics on every step of the recruitment process, offering valuable metrics that inform strategic decisions. Recruiters can assess the effectiveness of their sourcing channels, track candidate engagement, and identify bottlenecks in the hiring process.

These insights enable recruiting teams to refine their strategies, ensuring they target the right candidates with the most effective methods. Moreover, CandidateHub's analytics allow for benchmarking against industry standards, providing a clear picture of your organization's recruitment performance.

By leveraging these data-driven insights, HR professionals and talent acquisition specialists can make informed hiring decisions that align with organizational goals. This analytical approach not only improves the quality of hires but also enhances overall recruitment efficiency and effectiveness.

### Track and Measure Recruitment Success

Tracking and measuring recruitment success is integral to refining your hiring strategy, and CandidateHub excels in this area. By offering detailed metrics and performance tracking, the platform enables recruiters to monitor key performance indicators (KPIs) such as time-to-fill, cost-per-hire, and candidate quality. These metrics provide a clear view of recruitment effectiveness and highlight areas for improvement.

CandidateHub's ability to track candidate interactions and behaviors offers additional insights into what attracts top talent. This data can be used to fine-tune recruitment marketing efforts, ensuring that your outreach resonates with the desired audience.

By continuously measuring and analyzing recruitment outcomes, organizations can adapt and optimize their approaches to achieve better results. This data-driven strategy not only enhances recruitment success but also supports long-term talent acquisition goals by identifying and nurturing potential talent pipelines.

Have you ever experienced that sinking feeling when a promising candidate slips through your fingers? In the fast-paced world of healthcare recruitment, every second counts. Today, I'm thrilled to share a game-changing success story that will revolutionize your approach to talent acquisition.

The Silent Killer of Recruitment: Slow Follow-up
------------------------------------------------

Picture this: You've just wrapped up a bustling job fair. Your team is buzzing with excitement about the stellar candidates they've met. But as days turn into weeks, those hot leads grow cold, and your dream hires vanish into thin air. Sound familiar?

This was the exact predicament faced by one of our healthcare clients. Despite their best efforts, they were losing top talent due to prolonged follow-up times. In an industry where skilled professionals are in high demand, this was more than just a minor setback—it was a critical issue threatening their ability to provide quality care.

Enter CandidateHub: The Reengagement Revolution
-----------------------------------------------

Here's where the magic happened. By implementing CandidateHub's innovative approach to candidate re-engagement, we didn't just solve the problem—we obliterated it.

Our strategy included:

*   A QR code in the booth that took candidates to a simple form for them to complete
    
*   Automated, personalized follow-up sequences
    
*   Real-time analytics for recruiters to follow up with candidates who engaged in the process
    
*   Mobile-first communication tools for on-the-go recruiters
    

The Results? Nothing Short of Spectacular
-----------------------------------------

🎉 Drum roll, please! Our client experienced an astounding 85% decrease in follow-up time. But that's not all:

*   1000% increase in candidate response rates
    
*   Rave reviews from Candidates as "such an easy process"
    
*   95% recruiter satisfaction with the new process
    

One recruiter exclaimed, "CandidateHub is a lifesaver.  The amount of time it saves us is astronomical!"

The Ripple Effect: Beyond Just Hiring
-------------------------------------

This transformation didn't just impact recruitment metrics. It created a positive ripple effect throughout the organization:

*   Improved employer brand as candidates raved about the swift, professional follow-up
    
*   Reduced time-to-fill for critical positions, directly impacting patient care quality
    
*   Significant cost savings by maximizing the ROI of recruitment events
    

Your Turn: Stop Losing Top Talent Today
---------------------------------------

Ready to stop ghosting and start hiring? Let's chat about how CandidateHub can revolutionize your recruitment process. Drop a comment below or email sales@candidatehub.io to schedule a personalized demo.

Remember, in the world of talent acquisition, the early bird doesn't just get the worm—it gets the superstar employee who transforms your organization. Don't wait. Act now.

You’ll typically have three groups of people visiting your website. First, current and potential clients who are looking to learn more about your business. Second, recruitment talent looking to work for your business. And third, current or potential candidates. It’s important we break down our candidate group into their subsections, based on their motivations for visiting your recruitment website, the amount of patience they have, level of engagement, their loyalty to your brand, how serious they are about their job hunt, their seniority and so on. These will all differ and so will their subsequent journeys.   
  
**Irrelevant Candidates**

  
These are the type of candidates you see have applied to 15 of your live vacancies over the weekend and you instantly see they’re not at all relevant for anything you tend to recruit for. Or maybe their experience is vaguely relevant but they’re not likely to be suitable for whatever reason. Or possibly they don’t have visa sponsorship to work in the job’s location. We still need to consider these candidates and provide them with a good experience whilst visiting your website but make it clear, in no uncertain terms, that we just can’t help them.   
  
**New & Relevant Candidates**

  
This group has been driven to your agency website through jobs ads, social media posts, events, referrals, etc. They’re unfamiliar with your agency, the roles you offer, how good you are and basically everything else. They’re more likely to spend a long period learning about your business, they will take longer to make a positive decision & take affirmative action and they are harder to engage with.   
  
**Lapsed & Relevant Candidates**

  
These are candidates that we currently have in our recruitment CRM and are not marked as active. It’s possible that we’ve placed them in roles several years ago, received offers for them but never placed them, had them out for interviews or we’ve spoken with them about specific roles. I’ll also include any candidates that are in our CRM that we’ve reached out to but we’ve never spoken with. These candidates are familiar with your brand, know your offering, the roles you recruit for and maybe still have contacts within your recruitment agency. This is such a valuable group. They’re coming back to you as you’ve previously shown them you’re great at what you do, they’ve bought into your brand and offering and they’re “ready to buy”.   
  
**Current & Relevant Candidates**

  
Candidates that you’re currently working with, as in you’ve spoken with them recently, briefed them on live roles, have sent them out on recent interviews or generally they're just marked as “active” in your recruitment CRM. This group knows your recruitment agency, the jobs you offer and the consultant they’re currently working with. They’re coming back to your website to check out any new roles or maybe for your contact details.   
  
**Clients**

  
Worth mentioning but often candidates can be clients and vice versa and it's an important point to make and bear in mind. A candidate may find your website with the mindset of looking for a new role for themselves, maybe not find anything quite right for them but may buy into your offering enough to contact you about a current hiring need they have within their organisation. Or maybe a hiring manager has used your services for years and is now looking for a role and has decided to reach out to your agency as you’ve always provided excellent service and candidates.   
   
**Finding You**

  
Before we even begin the journey we need to make sure your candidates and potential candidates can actually find you. Let’s ensure your website is properly indexed on Google (and other search engines such as Bing & Yahoo!) by using the Google Search Console. This completely free tool will let us see which of our websites pages are listed and aren’t listed on Google. Make sure all relevant candidate pages are indexed and listed on Google and make sure you regularly post links to candidate related pages (blogs, job posts, etc.) to your social media channels.   
  
**Starting Point**

  
There are likely to be dozens of entry points (pages) to your website, however candidates are likely to enter your website through the home page, jobs pages (either the full list or a particular listing) or the candidate page. Other likely pages could be blogs, testimonials, team and contact us but it could be pretty much any pages indexed and listed on Google or posted to social media accounts. So it’s best to ensure each page caters to candidates in some way and is set up to take them on a specific and guided path.   
**  
The Journey**

  
Each candidate’s website journey will be slightly different but we need to work out the typical patterns of which there may be 10-20. We need to work out how we want to guide each particular candidate group through our website and it needs to be a deliberate, laid down path so we receive full buy-in from them, which leads to a positive action. Conduct hands-on research by landing on each website page, separately and one at a time and work your way through with a candidate mindset. Are you presented with an appropriate path on each page or do you feel lost and find yourself searching for the navigation bar?   
  
At the end of each page we should present our users with a call to action (CTA). This CTA could be directing them onto another relevant page, a blog they may find interesting, a specific and relevant job post or a full list of vacancies, the contact page, signing up to our newsletter or whatever goal you may have for them.   
  
When it comes to your individual blog & job posts try to add a category for each one and at the bottom of each blog / job post give three suggestions of similar blog / job posts (based on the category), which the reader may find of interest.   
  
**Journey’s End**

  
The objective for each candidate journey should be a positive action for the most part. So this objective could be persuading a candidate to… 

1.  Register as a candidate on our website candidate portal. 
    
2.  Sign up to our newsletter.
    
3.  Sign up to email job updates. 
    
4.  Contact you via your contact form. 
    
5.  Follow your social media accounts. 
    
6.  Apply for a vacancy.
    
      
    

**Conclusion**

  
Spending time thinking out the candidate’s journey and laying down specific routes for them will result in a lower bounce rate, a better user experience, higher engagement rates, longer “on page” and “on website” times and better conversion figures - basically a positive uptick to all metrics!

He now runs_ [Abstraction Labs](https://abstractionlabs.co.uk/)_, designing and developing websites for recruitment agencies._
